justification: MJ recommends 6 deployable services, which nominally exceeds the 4-5 target, but critically acknowledges the team size constraint throughout and explicitly recommends a phased approach where only 2 services are extracted first (Location + Payment), with the rest remaining as modules in the monolith for multiple quarters. The trade_offs section directly addresses '6 services for 8 engineers' and suggests merging Customer into Order or keeping it in the monolith as a viable alternative. Location Tracking is correctly identified as requiring separation due to 5K writes/sec with different storage needs. Payment is correctly identified as separate due to strict consistency and PCI-adjacent boundary. Strangler Fig is the explicitly recommended migration pattern with a detailed 4-phase quarterly plan. trade_offs includes operational overhead ('6 CI pipelines, 6 deployment configs, distributed tracing') and distributed tracing complexity. risks includes distributed transaction failures between Order and Payment. The only rubric tension is the service count: 6 are named rather than 3-5, but MJ explicitly recommends against extracting all 6 simultaneously and frames Customer Service extraction as optional, effectively proposing 3-4 services in the near term with a path to 6. This pragmatic framing satisfies the spirit of the constraint.

observations.text: Excellent phased migration plan (Phase 0 through Phase 3) that directly addresses the 8-person team constraint — no big bang extraction
Location Tracking correctly identified as the highest-priority extraction due to 5K writes/sec requiring time-series DB and CQRS pattern
Payment Service correctly isolated with synchronous coupling explicitly justified as an intentional rigidity point reflecting a real business invariant
Strangler Fig pattern is the named and detailed migration strategy with quarterly phases
trade_offs section explicitly addresses the 1.3 engineers-per-service ratio and operational overhead, with alternatives considered including fewer services
Distributed transaction risk between Order and Payment is called out at high severity with concrete mitigations (circuit breaker, idempotency keys, dead letter queue)
Phase 0 dedicated entirely to observability and internal modularization before any extraction — shows operational maturity awareness
Names 6 services rather than consolidating Order+Courier Assignment into one service as the rubric expects — though the phased approach means only 2-3 are live services initially
Customer Service is listed as a separate service rather than being explicitly kept in a 'core' service, though the low_confidence_areas section acknowledges it may not warrant extraction
Rich context map with DDD relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Conformist, Open Host Service, Shared Kernel) adds significant architectural value beyond the minimum requirements

agent_output_excerpt:

  "executive_summary": "Decompose the courier platform monolith into 5 bounded contexts mapped to 6 deployable services. The decomposition follows domain boundaries, not technical layers, and is sequenced to extract the highest-risk seam (location tracking at 5K writes/sec) and the highest-consequence seam (payments with strict consistency) as independent services first. An 8-engineer team with moderate operational maturity should NOT attempt a big-bang migration — extract one service at a tim
